name: local-dev-server
description: Zero-friction local development server management for Empathy Ledger using PM2
capabilities: Start local development server with PM2 process manager
Stop, restart, and monitor running server processes
Resolve port conflicts and address already in use errors
View centralized logs for multiple projects
Manage full ACT ecosystem with single commands
author: Acurioustractor
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown documentation. No executable code exists. All 154 static findings are false positives: the scanner misidentified SHA-256 hashes as weak crypto/C2 keywords, markdown code blocks as Ruby backticks, and legitimate PM2/lsof/xargs commands as dangerous external commands. This skill only provides guidance for using standard development tools.
valueStatement: Address already in use errors and server crashes disrupt development workflow. This skill provides PM2 commands to start, stop, and restart local development servers with auto-recovery and centralized logging.
userTitle: Start and manage local development servers
limitations: Requires PM2 to be installed globally via npm
Designed for macOS with specific path configurations
Does not configure PM2 ecosystem files automatically

useCases.title: Start multiple projects
useCases.description: Start all ACT ecosystem projects with a single command for comprehensive local development environment

useCases.title: Fix port conflicts
useCases.description: Resolve address already in use errors by killing processes and restarting servers cleanly

useCases.title: Monitor server health
useCases.description: View centralized logs and auto-restart crashed servers to maintain development continuity

promptTemplates.title: Start server
promptTemplates.prompt: Start the local development server for Empathy Ledger

promptTemplates.title: Fix port error
promptTemplates.prompt: Port 3030 is busy. Kill the existing process and restart the server.

promptTemplates.title: Restart after changes
promptTemplates.prompt: I updated the schema. Restart the dev server to pick up the changes.

promptTemplates.title: Full ecosystem
promptTemplates.prompt: Start all ACT ecosystem projects so I can work on the full platform.

bestPractices: Use ecosystem script to start all projects for consistent environment
Check server status with pm2 list before running tests
Save PM2 process list with pm2 save for session persistence
antiPatterns: Using npm run dev directly for production-like testing
Ignoring repeated server crashes without checking error logs
Leaving servers running overnight without pm2 save

faq.question: How do I stop the server?
faq.answer: Run pm2 stop empathy-ledger-solo to stop, then pm2 delete to remove from process list.

faq.question: What ports does this skill use?
faq.answer: Empathy Ledger uses port 3030 standalone or 3001 in ecosystem mode. Other ACT projects use ports 3002-3005 and 3999.

faq.question: Does this work on Linux?
faq.answer: PM2 commands are cross-platform. You will need to update paths in scripts from macOS to Linux format.

faq.question: Is my data safe with PM2?
faq.answer: Yes. PM2 only manages process lifecycle. It does not access, modify, or store your application data.

faq.question: Why does my server keep crashing?
faq.answer: Check logs with pm2 logs empathy-ledger --lines 50. Common causes are code errors, memory limits, or database connection issues.

faq.question: How is this different from npm run dev?
faq.answer: npm run dev is for active development with hot reload. PM2 provides persistence, auto-restart, and centralized logging for stable testing.
